scenario: Draft a plaint for recovery of Rs.15,00,000 given as a hand loan. The plaintiff Ram Kumar, aged 45, resident of Jayanagar, Bengaluru, advanced Rs.15,00,000 to the defendant Suresh Patel, aged 38, resident of Koramangala, Bengaluru, on 15.03.2024 via NEFT bank transfer (UTR: AXIB20240315123456). There was no written agreement. The defendant acknowledged the loan via WhatsApp messages. Despite repeated oral demands and a legal notice dated 01.07.2024, the defendant has failed to repay. The plaintiff claims interest at 12% per annum from the date of default (20.06.2024). File in City Civil Court, Bengaluru.

scorecard.detail: At least one statutory section cited

draft_text:
IN THE CITY CIVIL COURT AT BENGALURU (KARNATAKA)

O.S. No. {{SUIT_NUMBER}} OF 2024

Between:

Ram Kumar,
S/o {{PLAINTIFF_FATHER_NAME}},
Age: 45 years,
Occupation: {{PLAINTIFF_OCCUPATION}},
Residing at Jayanagar, Bengaluru, Karnataka – {{PLAINTIFF_PIN_CODE}}.

...Plaintiff

Versus

Suresh Patel,
S/o {{DEFENDANT_FATHER_NAME}},
Age: 38 years,
Occupation: {{DEFENDANT_OCCUPATION}},
Residing at Koramangala, Bengaluru, Karnataka – {{DEFENDANT_PIN_CODE}}.

...Defendant

PLAINT UNDER ORDER VII RULE 1 OF THE CODE OF CIVIL PROCEDURE, 1908 FOR RECOVERY OF MONEY

The Plaintiff respectfully submits as follows:

1. The Plaintiff is a resident of Jayanagar, Bengaluru. The Defendant is a resident of Koramangala, Bengaluru.

2. On 15.03.2024, the Plaintiff advanced a hand loan of Rs.15,00,000/- (Rupees Fifteen Lakhs only) to the Defendant by NEFT bank transfer bearing UTR No. AXIB20240315123456. The said transfer evidences advancement of the loan and creation of the Defendant’s obligation to repay the same.

3. There was no written agreement between the parties. The Defendant acknowledged receipt of the loan amount and the liability to repay through WhatsApp messages exchanged with the Plaintiff.

4. The loan was repayable on demand. Despite repeated oral demands by the Plaintiff, the Defendant failed to repay. For the purpose of the present claim, the Plaintiff treats 20.06.2024 as the date of default for computation of pre-suit interest.

5. The Plaintiff caused a legal notice dated 01.07.2024 to be issued to the Defendant calling upon him to repay the principal amount along with interest. The Defendant has failed to comply and has not repaid the amount.

6. The Defendant has failed and neglected to repay Rs.15,00,000/- and is liable to pay the said amount to the Plaintiff along with interest and costs.

LEGAL BASIS

7. The Defendant is bound to repay the hand loan advanced by the Plaintiff under the oral understanding between the parties, as acknowledged in the WhatsApp messages.

8. This Hon’ble Court has territorial jurisdiction as the Defendant resides within Bengaluru and the cause of action arose within Bengaluru.

9. Interest pendente lite and future interest may be awarded under Section 34 of the Code of Civil Procedure, 1908. The Plaintiff claims pre-suit interest at 12% per annum as reasonable compensation for wrongful retention/non-payment after default.

10. Costs may be awarded under Section 35 of the Code of Civil Procedure, 1908.

CAUSE OF ACTION

11. The cause of action arose on 15.03.2024 when the Plaintiff advanced the hand loan of Rs.15,00,000/- to the Defendant by NEFT (UTR: AXIB20240315123456).

12. The cause of action further arose on 20.06.2024 when, despite demands, the Defendant failed to repay and defaulted.

13. The cause of action further/last arose on 01.07.2024 when the Plaintiff caused issuance of a legal notice demanding repayment and the Defendant failed to comply.

LIMITATION

14. The suit is governed by Article 21 of the Limitation Act, 1963.

15. The prescribed period is three years, and the time begins to run when the loan is made. The present suit is within limitation.

JURISDICTION

16. This Hon’ble Court has territorial jurisdiction under Section 20 of the Code of Civil Procedure, 1908 as the Defendant resides within Bengaluru and the cause of action arose within Bengaluru.

17. This Hon’ble Court has pecuniary jurisdiction to try the suit as the value of the claim falls within the competent limits of the City Civil Court, Bengaluru.

VALUATION AND COURT FEE

18. The suit is valued at Rs.15,00,000/- for the purposes of jurisdiction and court fee.

19. The requisite court fee as per the Karnataka Court Fees and Suits Valuation Act, 1958, as applicable, is paid on the plaint.

INTEREST

20. The Plaintiff claims pre-suit interest at 12% per annum on Rs.15,00,000/- from 20.06.2024 till the date of filing of the suit.

21. The Plaintiff claims pendente lite interest at such rate as this Hon’ble Court deems fit from the date of filing of the suit till the date of decree under Section 34 of the Code of Civil Procedure, 1908.

22. The Plaintiff claims future interest at such rate as this Hon’ble Court deems fit from the date of decree till realisation under Section 34 of the Code of Civil Procedure, 1908.

PRAYER

Wherefore, the Plaintiff prays that this Hon’ble Court be pleased to pass a judgment and decree in favour of the Plaintiff and against the Defendant:

(a) directing the Defendant to pay to the Plaintiff a sum of Rs.15,00,000/- (Rupees Fifteen Lakhs only) being the principal amount of the hand loan;

(b) awarding pre-suit interest at the rate of 12% per annum on Rs.15,00,000/- from 20.06.2024 till the date of filing of the suit;

(c) awarding pendente lite interest at such rate as this Hon’ble Court deems fit from the date of filing of the suit till the date of decree under Section 34 of the Code of Civil Procedure, 1908;

(d) awarding future interest at such rate as this Hon’ble Co
